Package org.eventb.core.pm

Interface IProofAttempt

All Known Implementing Classes:
ProofAttempt
public interface IProofAttempt
Common protocol for proof attempts. A proof attempt represents a proof obligation that some client is attempting to discharge. Proof attempts are created from proof components with the createProofAttempt method.

From a proof attempt, a proof tree can be obtained. Just after creation, the proof tree is empty and its root is the sequent of the corresponding proof obligation. Then, various tactics can be applied to this proof tree. When the proof attempt is committed, the proof is extracted from the proof tree and saved to the proof file. The corresponding proof status is updated accordingly. A proof attempt can be committed several times.

If the proof obligation corresponding with a proof attempt changes during a proof attempt lifetime, then the proof attempt is considered as broken. However, this does not prevent the proof attempt from being committed (the only impact is that the resulting proof status will be marked as broken).

Once a proof attempt is not needed anymore, it must be disposed so that the memory used by it and its proof tree can be recovered.

A proof attempt can be in one of the two following state:

LIVE
This is the initial state in which a proof attempt can be safely manipulated and committed to the proof files
DISPOSED
This is the final state in which a proof attempt cannot be committed anymore. All changes to the proof tree will thus be lost.
A proof attempt state can only move from LIVE to DISPOSED. There is no way back.

Within a proof component, a proof attempt is uniquely identified by the name of the corresponding proof obligation and its owner.

This interface is not intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IProofComponent.createProofAttempt(String, String, IProgressMonitor)

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    commit(boolean manual, boolean simplify, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Commits this proof attempts to the proof and proof status files of this proof component.
    void
    commit(boolean manual, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor)
    Commits this proof attempts to the proof and proof status files of this proof component.
    void
    dispose()
    Disposes this proof attempt.
    IProofComponent
    getComponent()
    Returns the proof component from which this proof attempt was created.
    FormulaFactory
    getFormulaFactory()
    Returns the formula factory for this proof attempt.
    String
    getName()
    Returns the name of the proof obligation for which this proof attempt was created.
    String
    getOwner()
    Returns the owner of this proof attempt, i.e., the string that was passed to createProofAttempt() when creating this proof attempt..
    IProofTree
    getProofTree()
    Returns the proof tree of this proof attempt.
    IPSStatus
    getStatus()
    Returns a handle to the proof status associated with this proof attempt.
    boolean
    isBroken()
    Tells whether this proof attempt is still in sync with the corresponding proof obligation.
    boolean
    isDisposed()
    Tells whether this proof attempt has been disposed.

  • Method Details

    • commit

      void commit(boolean manual, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Commits this proof attempts to the proof and proof status files of this proof component. If this proof attempt is broken, then proof status after commit will also be marked as broken.

      Note that committing a proof attempt only modifies the in-memory copy of the corresponding files. These files will then be considered as containing pending changes by the Rodin database and will need to be saved to make the change persistent. They can be saved using the save method of the proof component.

      This proof attempt must not have been disposed yet.

      This is a long running operation which is scheduled using the rule of the proof component of this proof attempt.

      This is equivalent to calling commit(boolean, boolean, IProgressMonitor) with a simplify argument set to false.

      Parameters:
      manual - true iff this proof attempt is manual
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this proof attempt has been disposed prior to this call
      RodinDBException - if there is some problem committing this proof attempt to the Rodin database
      See Also:
      • IProofComponent.getSchedulingRule()
      • IProofComponent.save(IProgressMonitor, boolean)
      • IPRProof.getHasManualProof()
      • IPSStatus.isBroken()

    • commit

      void commit(boolean manual, boolean simplify, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Commits this proof attempts to the proof and proof status files of this proof component. If this proof attempt is broken, then proof status after commit will also be marked as broken.

      Note that committing a proof attempt only modifies the in-memory copy of the corresponding files. These files will then be considered as containing pending changes by the Rodin database and will need to be saved to make the change persistent. They can be saved using the save method of the proof component.

      This proof attempt must not have been disposed yet.

      This is a long running operation which is scheduled using the rule of the proof component of this proof attempt.

      Parameters:
      manual - true iff this proof attempt is manual
      simplify - whether to simplify the proof tree before saving it
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this proof attempt has been disposed prior to this call
      RodinDBException - if there is some problem committing this proof attempt to the Rodin database
      See Also:
      • IProofComponent.getSchedulingRule()
      • IProofComponent.save(IProgressMonitor, boolean)
      • IPRProof.getHasManualProof()
      • IPSStatus.isBroken()

    • dispose

      void dispose()
      Disposes this proof attempt. Once disposed, this proof attempt cannot be committed anymore. Disposing a proof attempt which has already been disposed has no effect.

    • getComponent

      IProofComponent getComponent()
      Returns the proof component from which this proof attempt was created.
      Returns:
      the proof component of this proof attempt

    • getName

      String getName()
      Returns the name of the proof obligation for which this proof attempt was created.
      Returns:
      the name of this proof attempt

    • getOwner

      String getOwner()
      Returns the owner of this proof attempt, i.e., the string that was passed to createProofAttempt() when creating this proof attempt..
      Returns:
      the owner of this proof attempt

    • getFormulaFactory

      FormulaFactory getFormulaFactory()
      Returns the formula factory for this proof attempt.
      Returns:
      a formula factory
      Since:
      2.0

    • getProofTree

      IProofTree getProofTree()
      Returns the proof tree of this proof attempt. The proof tree can be manipulated freely by clients. Upon creation, the proof tree only contains a root node, the sequent of which is the proof obligation of this proof attempt.
      Returns:
      the proof tree of this proof attempt

    • getStatus

      IPSStatus getStatus()
      Returns a handle to the proof status associated with this proof attempt. The proof status is the database element that will be updated when this proof attempt gets committed.
      Returns:
      the proof status associated with this proof attempt.

    • isBroken

      boolean isBroken() throws RodinDBException
      Tells whether this proof attempt is still in sync with the corresponding proof obligation. This proof attempt is considered as broken if the corresponding proof obligation has changed since the creation of this proof attempt.
      Returns:
      true iff the proof obligation has changed
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there is some problem accessing the proof obligation (other than the fact that the proof obligation does not exist anymore)

    • isDisposed

      boolean isDisposed()
      Tells whether this proof attempt has been disposed.
      Returns:
      true iff this proof attempt has been disposed
